root_cause.summary: The committed-carry path exposed a latent JAX-pytree-contract violation: State.tree_unflatten and DycoreMetrics.tree_unflatten re-ran their constructors (cls(*children) / cls(**values)), which CANONICALISE array children (State.__init__ does jnp.asarray(lu_index, dtype=int32) and None->zeros_like defaults; DycoreMetrics.__post_init__ casts every metric to fp64). JAX legitimately calls tree_unflatten with a non-array placeholder of type 'Leaf' when it FORMATS a treedef-mismatch message (jax._src.tree_util.equality_errors_pytreedef builds tree_unflatten(td, [Leaf]*n)). Routing that Leaf through jnp.asarray(..., dtype=...) raised 'TypeError: int()/float() argument ... not Leaf' and aborted lax.scan tracing in _advance_chunk before any compute. The committed initial carry (jax.device_put of the whole OperationalCarry) changed leaf shardings/avals enough that JAX took the mismatch-reporting branch, where the un-tolerant tree_unflatten crashed; the uncommitted path happened not to take that branch.
root_cause.leaf_sentinel_source: jax/_src/tree_util.py:762 equality_errors_pytreedef builds a metaclass instance named 'Leaf'

fix.change: Made both tree_unflatten methods the EXACT structural inverse of tree_flatten: object.__new__(cls) + object.__setattr__ to assign the flattened leaves verbatim, bypassing constructor canonicalisation. The stored leaves are already canonical (dtype-correct), so this is value-preserving, and it tolerates arbitrary placeholder leaves (Leaf sentinels) so JAX can format mismatch messages without crashing. Also resolves the separately-tracked PROOF_TABLE row-11 '.lower()-safe State.tree_unflatten' blocker.
fix.note: The recompile prevention itself (explicit-device-commit of the initial carry + stable _StaticHolder(None) hash) from 77700d9 is retained unchanged; this commit only fixes the tracing crash it introduced.

1_no_recompile_per_chunk.evidence: JAX_LOG_COMPILES=1 3x180-step d02 run: exactly ONE 'Compiling jit(_advance_chunk)' and ONE _advance_chunk TRACING CACHE MISS, both during chunk 1; chunks 2 and 3 reuse the cached executable (only the one-time _m9_snapshot diagnostic compiles after the chunks).

2_hot_chunk_time.interpretation: chunk1 cold (3.24s trace + 114.2s XLA compile); chunks 2-3 HOT ~11.83s each (no recompile), at/below the ~12s target.

3_bit_identity_vs_baseline.method: 1h d02 forecast via run_forecast_operational_with_m9_diagnostics on the fixed branch vs the SAME harness on a clean d054954 worktree (baseline canonicalising tree_unflatten). sha256 over all 56 State leaves + 10 M9 diagnostics.
